verdict: 2026-07-17 11:59:50 PDT — Sound, modern design. The one-line declarative CSP change directly satisfies the acceptance criteria, matches the repository’s existing CSP and native node:test conventions, and keeps the policy invariant centralized in the exported CSP array. Adding Helmet or a CSP-building abstraction would introduce dependency and migration cost without eliminating a meaningful error path for this narrowly scoped change. The proposed directive-bounded served-header assertions are appropriate; the live in-Zoom smoke test correctly owns the only material residual compatibility risk.
